candidate_llm_application: AFAR-CARAVAN-CASCADE — multi-segment LLM agent cascade with falloff-redistribution + cross-segment redundancy + compressed handoff. K agents A_1..A_K each with payload capacity B tokens; hand-off P_{i+1}=compress(P_i ∪ A_i additions, B); verification gate at segment-end; on failure of A_i, payload PARTIALLY REDISTRIBUTED across A_{i+1}..A_K via top-up rather than dropped; shared route-memory log.
rule: hit iff kw_count>=2 OR sem_score>=0.7 OR func_score>=0.7; total_hits>=1 => FAIL

results.title: Resilient Write 6-Layer 2604.10842 — task-continuity handoff envelopes + out-of-band scratchpad + structured errors
results.rationale: Resilient Write 6-Layer 2604.10842 supplies the EXACT segment-cascade-with-recovery pattern AFAR-CARAVAN-CASCADE replicates. Its 6 layers — pre-flight risk + atomic writes + resume-safe chunking + structured errors + out-of-band scratchpad + task-continuity handoff envelopes — together IMPLEMENT (a) compressed segment-to-segment handoff (= task-continuity handoff envelopes), (b) verification gate at segment-end (= pre-flight risk + structured errors), (c) shared route-memory log (= out-of-band scratchpad), and (d) partial-payload recovery across segments (= resume-safe chunking). The candidate's only delta is renaming 'chunking + resume on failure' as 'falloff-load-redistribution top-up to surviving agents A_{i+1}..A_K', which is the same operational pattern of redistributing the failed chunk's residual work across remaining segments. Functional-twin on the cascade backbone, semantic-twin on the verification + handoff envelope substrate.

results.title: Faulty-Agent Resilience OpenReview bkiM54QftZ — Challenger + Inspector recover 96.4% errors from faulty agents
results.rationale: EXACT-TWIN on the cross-segment-redundancy + failed-agent-recovery claim. Faulty-Agent Resilience OpenReview bkiM54QftZ establishes that Challenger + Inspector mechanisms recover 96.4% of errors from faulty agents in multi-agent collaboration — IS the mutual-protection + verification-gate + recovery-on-failure stack AFAR-CARAVAN-CASCADE assembles. The candidate's verification gate at segment-end maps to Inspector; the falloff-redistribution top-up to A_{i+1}..A_K maps to Challenger error-recovery. The 96.4% recovery rate already covers the operational regime claimed. Cross-segment redundancy / mutual protection is precisely the Challenger+Inspector failure-handling paradigm under a different label.

results.title: Galileo Multi-Agent Failure Recovery — circuit-breakers + token-budget limits
results.rationale: Galileo Multi-Agent Failure Recovery directly supplies the (a) circuit-breaker = verification-gate-on-failure and (b) token-budget = fixed-payload-capacity-B-tokens primitives AFAR-CARAVAN-CASCADE composes. The 'production-grade multi-agent failure recovery' framing IS the engineering operationalization of falloff-redistribution: a circuit-breaker trips on A_i failure and re-routes work under the remaining budget across surviving agents. Functional-twin on the budget + circuit-breaker + recovery composition; semantic-twin on the multi-agent-system failure-recovery framing.

results.title: TradingAgents — LangGraph multi-stage handoff + checkpoint resume
results.rationale: TradingAgents already implements the multi-stage agent pipeline with explicit per-stage handoff and LangGraph checkpoint-resume — exactly the segment-cascade-with-compressed-handoff backbone. The candidate explicitly contrasts itself with TradingAgents (citing 'no falloff-redistribution'), but checkpoint-resume IS the redistribution primitive: on stage failure, the checkpoint is reloaded and the remaining stages continue under updated state — operationally equivalent to topping up surviving agents from the failed segment's residual payload. The 'analyst→trader→portfolio' pipeline is the same K-agent A_1..A_K cascade pattern.

results.title: CHAP NDSS 2026 — shift-based agents passing compressed session state
results.rationale: CHAP NDSS 2026 IS the compressed-state segment-to-segment handoff paradigm AFAR-CARAVAN-CASCADE labels as its core mechanism. Shift-based agents passing compressed session state directly instantiates P_{i+1} = compress(P_i ∪ A_i additions, B): each shift produces a compressed session-state envelope handed to the next shift, with bounded payload capacity. The candidate's claim of differentiation 'no redistribution' is undermined by CHAP's inherent shift-handover-on-failure semantic — when a shift agent hits a quality bound, the residual investigation is by construction handed to subsequent shifts. Functional-twin on compressed-state-handoff and shift-cascade.

results.title: LangChain Handoffs — explicit per-segment handoff structured state
results.rationale: LangChain Handoffs documents the canonical multi-agent explicit per-segment handoff protocol with structured state — the production-deployment instance of the compressed-state segment-to-segment handoff AFAR-CARAVAN-CASCADE claims. LangChain Handoffs supports structured state + per-handoff filters + size limits, covering the candidate's fixed-payload-capacity-B-tokens and the compress-on-handoff primitive. The candidate's stated delta ('without per-segment redundancy + redistribution') is a wrapper preference, not a new mechanism, since LangChain's structured-state handoff is the substrate on which redundancy is trivially layered.

results.title: MAST 2503.13657 — 14-failure-mode multi-agent taxonomy
results.rationale: MAST 2503.13657 catalogues 14 failure modes in multi-agent LLM systems including the exact segment-failure → handoff-corruption → cascade-collapse modes AFAR-CARAVAN-CASCADE aims to mitigate. The taxonomy enumerates verification-gate-bypass, lossy-compression-handoff, partial-payload-loss-on-agent-failure as named failure modes — IS the diagnostic frame in which the candidate's mechanism is positioned. The 'verification gate at segment-end + redistribution on failure' design responds to MAST's already-named cascade-collapse mode; the response pattern (gate + recover) is itself part of MAST's recommended mitigation cluster.

results.title: Swarm Routing Nature 2026 — swarm orchestration + routing + controlled handoffs
results.rationale: Swarm Routing Nature 2026 supplies swarm orchestration + routing + controlled handoffs + shared state — directly covers cross-segment redundancy + shared route-memory log + segment-to-segment routing. The candidate explicitly cites Swarm Routing 2026 as differing ('swarm coordination, not segment-cascade-with-falloff'), but routing-with-failure-aware-rebalancing is a standard feature of swarm orchestration: when one swarm node degrades, work is rebalanced across surviving nodes — precisely the falloff-load-redistribution mechanism. Shared state across swarm IS the shared route-memory log. Functional-twin on the swarm-rebalancing-on-failure pattern.

reason: All 8 results trigger hit under sem>=0.7 AND func>=0.7. The candidate decomposes exhaustively across four prior-art clusters: (cluster A — segment-cascade-with-compressed-handoff backbone) CHAP NDSS 2026 (sem 0.86, func 0.84) supplies compressed session-state shift-handoff = P_{i+1}=compress(P_i ∪ A_i additions, B); LangChain Handoffs (sem 0.80, func 0.78) supplies explicit per-segment structured-state handoff with size limits; TradingAgents (sem 0.82, func 0.80) supplies the K-agent LangGraph pipeline + checkpoint-resume; (cluster B — verification-gate + failure-recovery) Faulty-Agent Resilience bkiM54QftZ (sem 0.92, func 0.92) supplies Challenger+Inspector verification+recovery at 96.4% recovery rate = verification gate + falloff redistribution; Resilient Write 6-Layer 2604.10842 (sem 0.90, func 0.90) supplies pre-flight risk + structured errors + resume-safe chunking + handoff envelopes — IS the verification+recovery+compressed-handoff stack the candidate claims; (cluster C — production failure-recovery patterns) Galileo (sem 0.88, func 0.86) supplies circuit-breakers + token-budget limits = the engineering operationalization of fixed-payload-B + verification-gate-trip; (cluster D — diagnostic + orchestration substrate) MAST 2503.13657 (sem 0.78, func 0.76) names the cascade-failure modes the mechanism mitigates; Swarm Routing Nature 2026 (sem 0.76, func 0.74) supplies swarm-rebalancing + shared-state-log = cross-segment redundancy + route-memory. The three claimed contributions — (a) fixed-payload compressed handoff, (b) falloff-redistribution on segment failure, (c) shared route-memory log — are anticipated cluster-by-cluster: (a) by CHAP + LangChain Handoffs + TradingAgents, (b) by bkiM54QftZ Challenger+Inspector + Resilient Write 2604.10842 resume-safe chunking + Galileo circuit-breakers, (c) by Resilient Write out-of-band scratchpad + Swarm Routing shared state. The Afar Danakil salt-caravan / multi-trader mutual-protection / salt-brick-load-distribution cultural framing supplies only narrative for the cascade + redundancy framing; the underlying mechanism is fully covered. total_hits = 8 >> 1, distinct_effect_clusters = 4 >> 1, candidate FAILS.
recommendation: REJECT candidate. AFAR-CARAVAN-CASCADE is fully anticipated by the conjunction Resilient Write 6-Layer 2604.10842 + Faulty-Agent Resilience bkiM54QftZ + CHAP NDSS 2026 + Galileo Multi-Agent Failure Recovery, with TradingAgents + LangChain Handoffs + Swarm Routing 2026 supplying the cascade + handoff + routing substrate and MAST 2503.13657 supplying the failure-mode taxonomy. Specifically: (1) the K-segment compressed-handoff pipeline P_{i+1}=compress(P_i ∪ A_i additions, B) is the CHAP shift-compressed-session-state + LangChain structured-handoff + TradingAgents LangGraph checkpoint pattern; (2) the segment-end verification gate + on-failure redistribution is the Challenger+Inspector 96.4% recovery loop of bkiM54QftZ AND the Resilient Write 2604.10842 pre-flight risk + structured errors + resume-safe chunking stack AND the Galileo circuit-breaker + budget-limit pattern; (3) the shared route-memory log is the Resilient Write out-of-band scratchpad and the Swarm Routing shared-state ledger; (4) the failure modes the candidate addresses are pre-catalogued in MAST 14-failure-mode taxonomy with the gate+recover mitigation already in MAST's recommended response cluster. To salvage, the candidate would need a feature uniquely supplied by the Afar salt-caravan source not anticipated by these clusters, e.g.: (a) a Danakil 'salt-brick crystalline load' constraint encoded as cryptographically-attested fixed-mass payload units where each agent A_i mints a hashable salt-brick = irreducible token-bundle that cannot be split mid-handoff (defeating partial-corruption attacks that current LangChain/CHAP handoffs allow); (b) a multi-trader 'mutual protection assembly' operationalized as a k-of-K secret-sharing scheme over the route-memory log such that NO subset of agents below threshold can read the full route history while ANY K-failure-tolerant subset can reconstruct it, providing both confidentiality + redundancy beyond Resilient Write's plain scratchpad; (c) a 'caravan-ahara waypoint' constraint forcing each segment's verification gate to commit to a Merkle-root of the cumulative compressed payload, with each subsequent agent required to prove inclusion of all prior verified states — defeating cascade-replay attacks not covered by MAST mitigations; (d) an 'Afar Depression altitude gradient' operationalized as a monotonically-tightening verification threshold per segment depth (early agents lax, late agents strict), reflecting the highland-market quality bar, with formal bounds on cumulative compression loss as a function of depth. As stated, AFAR-CARAVAN-CASCADE is in the saturated region documented by 2604.10842 + bkiM54QftZ + CHAP + Galileo + TradingAgents + LangChain Handoffs + Swarm Routing 2026, with MAST supplying the failure-mode catalog the mechanism merely indexes against.
